story.iWant: to describe layout changes in plain English
story.soThat: I can modify slide structure without technical knowledge

task.name: Implement Layout Regenerator Module
task.subtask: Load theme.json for style consistency during regeneration
Prepare frontend-design skill invocation with current HTML, instruction, theme, constraints, preserve_fields list
Invoke frontend-design skill with prepared context
Receive new HTML layout from skill
Validate response has contenteditable, data-field, theme CSS variables, 1920x1080 dimensions

task.name: Implement Current Slide Reading
task.subtask: Read current slide HTML from target path (determined in Story 4.1)
Parse HTML to extract all data-field values and their current content
Read slide-state.json to get all saved text edits
Merge browser localStorage edits with state file (if applicable)
Build complete edit collection: [{selector, content, lastModified}]

task.name: Implement Edit Reapplicator Module
task.subtask: For each saved edit, search new HTML for element matching data-field selector
If matching element found: replace innerHTML with saved content, mark "applied"
If no matching element found: mark as "orphaned", preserve in state file
Ensure auto-save JavaScript is present in final HTML
Test multiple edit cycles preserve accumulated edits

task.name: Implement Slide Persister Module
task.subtask: Write final HTML to original slide file location
Update slide-state.json with edits, lastModified, regenerationCount
Update status.yaml with last_action and history entry

task.name: Update Edit Workflow Instructions
task.subtask: Extend instructions.md: Add Phase 4 (Layout Regeneration), Phase 5 (Edit Reapplication), Phase 6 (Save and Report)
Update workflow.yaml if needed for new phase configuration
Ensure workflow handles both single mode and deck mode consistently

task.name: Testing - Edit Preservation Verification
task.subtask: Test: Edit slide title, regenerate layout, verify title edit preserved
Test: Edit 3 different fields, regenerate, verify all 3 preserved
Test: Multiple sequential /edit commands, verify cumulative edits preserved
Test: Verify theme CSS variables present in regenerated slide
Test: Verify status.yaml updated with edit action
Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given a slide is loaded for editing, when the user describes a layout change (e.g., "Move the diagram to the right"), then the system reads current slide HTML
The system reads existing text edits from slide-state.json before regeneration
The system regenerates the layout based on the instruction using frontend-design skill
The system preserves all user text edits by reapplying them to matching data-field selectors
The regenerated slide maintains theme consistency (uses theme CSS variables)
The user text content is not lost during layout regeneration
Given the user makes multiple edit requests, when each request is processed, then previous text edits continue to persist
The regenerated slide is saved to the original slide file location
status.yaml is updated with last_action describing the edit operation

docs.doc: Complete technical specification including Layout Regenerator module, Edit Reapplicator module, data models (slide-state.json schema, edit_context schema), and complete /edit workflow Phases 4-6.
Defines the edit preservation pattern: read state → generate → reapply edits → save. Uses data-field selectors to match content across layouts. Never delete state entries, only add/update.
Layout regeneration uses frontend-design skill for novel layouts. Skill receives full theme.json for brand consistency.
Required HTML structure: contenteditable="true" on all text elements, data-field attributes, theme CSS variables in :root, 1920x1080 dimensions.
Previous story implementation learnings: edit workflow structure, mode detection pattern, slide loader pattern, state file schema.

file.reason: Edit workflow configuration v2.0 - defines all path variables and command interface. Story 4.2 extends this workflow.
Current edit workflow instructions implementing Phases 1-7. Story 4.2 enhances Phase 5 (Apply Edit) with actual frontend-design skill invocation and edit reapplication.
Test slide with contenteditable elements and data-field attributes. Story 4.2 regenerates this file while preserving edits.
file.notes: Has 3 contenteditable fields: title, subtitle, logo. Has auto-save JavaScript script. Uses theme colors (#CCFF00 primary, #5DBAB6 secondary, #1A1A1A dark bg).

file.reason: Test state file with 2 saved edits. Story 4.2 reads this before regeneration and preserves edits after.
file.notes: Schema: { slide, edits: [{selector, content}], lastModified }. Story 4.2 adds orphanedEdits array and regenerationCount.

file.reason: Complete brand theme with colors, typography, shapes, components. Required for style consistency during regeneration.
file.notes: Amperity brand: primary=#CCFF00 (lime), secondary=#5DBAB6 (teal), font=Outfit. Bold-minimal personality.

file.reason: Global status tracking. Story 4.2 updates last_action and history after edit operation.
file.notes: Current mode: single. built_count: 1. Contains action history array.

package.name: puppeteer
package.purpose: HTML-to-image conversion (Epic 7, not used in Story 4.2)

package.name: googleapis
package.purpose: Google Slides API (Epic 7, not used in Story 4.2)

tool.name: frontend-design skill
tool.purpose: Regenerate slide layouts based on natural language instruction. CRITICAL for Story 4.2.

constraints.constraint: Regenerated slides MUST maintain all contenteditable and data-field attributes on text elements
State file edits MUST be preserved even if no matching selector exists (mark as orphaned, do not delete)
Regenerated slides MUST use theme CSS variables consistently (inject from theme.json)
Layout changes MUST be processed by frontend-design skill for quality assurance
Regeneration should feel responsive (target less than 30 seconds)
Auto-save JavaScript MUST be present in final HTML
All file operations are local-first (no external API calls except skill invocation)

interface.name: frontend-design skill invocation
interface.signature: Invoke frontend-design skill with edit_context object containing: slide_path, current_html, instruction, theme, constraints (dimensions, required_attributes, preserve_fields)

interface.name: slide-state.json
interface.signature: { slide: string, edits: [{selector: string, content: string, lastModified?: string}], orphanedEdits?: [{selector, content, orphanedAt, reason}], lastModified: string, regenerationCount?: number }

interface.name: status.yaml history entry
interface.signature: { action: string, timestamp: string, preserved_edits?: number, orphaned_edits?: number, regeneration_duration_ms?: number }

interface.name: Edit Reapplicator Algorithm
interface.signature: For each edit in saved_edits: find element by data-field selector → if found: replace innerHTML, mark applied → if not found: mark orphaned, preserve in state

tests.standards: Per Architecture test strategy and Tech Spec test approach: Story 4.2 uses Frontend Test Gate pattern with manual browser testing. No automated unit tests - validation through functional testing of the complete edit workflow. Test scenarios cover edit preservation, layout regeneration, theme consistency, and status updates.

ideas.idea: Test: Run /sb:edit on existing slide, verify current HTML is read before regeneration (check console output)
Test: Create 3 text edits in browser, run /sb:edit, verify all 3 edits read from state file before regeneration
Test: Request "add subtitle" via /sb:edit, verify frontend-design skill is invoked and returns new HTML
Test: Edit title in browser to "My Custom Title", regenerate layout, verify title still shows "My Custom Title"
Test: Inspect regenerated slide HTML for :root CSS variables matching theme.json (--color-primary: #CCFF00, etc.)
Test: Edit 3 fields, regenerate, inspect HTML - all 3 custom texts must be present
Test: Run /sb:edit twice with different instructions, verify all edits from both sessions preserved
Test: After /sb:edit, verify .slide-builder/single/slide.html file is updated (check file timestamp)
Test: After /sb:edit, verify status.yaml has new history entry with "Layout edited:" action
